chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-1511 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 13.01.2026 Mr. Muntazir Mehdi, advocate for petitioner Mr. Amjad Ali advocate a/w Senior Accounts Officer HMC Farhan Khan Dayo Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional A.G Sindh *** Pursuant to the Order dated 09.12.2025, the Senior Accounts Officer, Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (HMC), has filed a statement dated 13.01.2026. It is stated therein that the total outstanding liability payable to the petitioner amounts to Rs. 33,25,791/-, comprising Rs.2,93,515/- towards gratuity and Rs. 30,32,276/- towards family pension for the period 08.01.2011 to 31.12.2025. It is further stated that the aforesaid amount shall be released in six equal instalments. In compliance, the Senior Accounts Officer has produced the original cheque No. 24558070, dated 01.01.2026, amounting to Rs. 5,55,000/-, being the first instalment. However, he submits that the petitioner has not yet provided verified bank account details. The Additional Registrar is directed to retain the said original cheque in safe custody and hand it over to the petitioner upon his approach, subject to due verification and identification. Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that the amounts pertaining to Group Insurance and Financial Assistance are still outstanding. Upon query, the Senior Accounts Officer submits that matters relating to Group Insurance fall within the domain of the State Life Insurance Corporation and that if the petitioner submits an application, the same shall be forwarded to the competent authority within two days for a decision strictly in accordance with law.

Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that the amounts pertaining to Group Insurance and Financial Assistance are still outstanding. Upon query, the Senior Accounts Officer submits that matters relating to Group Insurance fall within the domain of the State Life Insurance Corporation and that if the petitioner submits an application, the same shall be forwarded to the competent authority within two days for a decision strictly in accordance with law. Regarding Financial Assistance, the Senior Accounts Officer undertakes that a formal communication shall be addressed to the Finance Department, Government of Sindh, within one week, for processing and release of the amount, if the petitioner is found entitled under the applicable rules. Learned counsel for the petitioner expresses satisfaction with the statement and undertakings furnished by the Senior Accounts Officer and seeks the disposal of the petition. Accordingly, and in view of the above undertakings, this petition stands disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar
